REFERENCE GENES IN Heliconius erato lativitta

1.2. Ct Boxplot

I made changes in this part, because we agreed to split the information in three boxplots i.e sex, tissue and treatment

1.3. Ct Distribution

1.3.1. Ct Homogeneity of Variance (Levene Test)

Tests whether Ct variance is homogeneous across Treatment × Sex × Tissue for each gene. A significant result (p < 0.05) indicates heteroscedasticity — relevant for model selection.

Levene Test — Ct homogeneity of variance by gene
Gene F_value Pr_F Signif
ACT1 4.708e-01 9.506e-01 ns
ACT2 5.719e-01 8.907e-01 ns
AK 5.596e-01 8.993e-01 ns
ANX 5.839e-01 8.819e-01 ns
eEfa1 7.040e-01 7.782e-01 ns
pAbp 7.324e-01 7.505e-01 ns
RPL3 4.043e-01 9.755e-01 ns
GAPDH 5.794e-01 8.852e-01 ns
UCCR 6.980e-01 7.839e-01 ns

1.4. Ct GLM Analysis

Model specification: Ct ~ predictors with family selected automatically by AIC comparison in section 1.4.0 (consensus across all genes). Type II Likelihood Ratio (LR) tests are used for all terms.

1.4.0. GLM Family Selection

The optimal GLM family for Ct is selected per gene by comparing AIC across three candidate specifications fitted with the additive formula. The consensus family (winner in most genes) is then used automatically in all hypothesis-testing models (sections 1.4.1–1.4.3). This table is intended for Supplementary Material.

Candidate families: Gamma(log), Gamma(inverse), Gaussian(identity). All are valid because Ct values are strictly positive (range: 14.3 – 38.8). The winning AIC per gene is highlighted in green.

Supplementary — Family selection AIC per gene (additive model)
Gene Gamma(log) Gamma(inverse) Gaussian(identity) Best
ACT1 275.35 274.32 278.16 Gamma(inverse)
ACT2 285.4 284.58 287.68 Gamma(inverse)
AK 284.95 284.21 287.89 Gamma(inverse)
ANX 279.43 278.56 282.11 Gamma(inverse)
eEfa1 284.66 284.1 288.71 Gamma(inverse)
pAbp 287.12 286.98 287.79 Gamma(inverse)
RPL3 259.98 260.07 263.69 Gamma(log)
GAPDH 296.71 297.25 303.58 Gamma(log)
UCCR 263.76 263.43 264.57 Gamma(inverse)

Consensus family selected: Gamma(inverse)
(7 of 9 genes). All models in sections 1.4.1–1.4.3 use this family automatically.

Heatmap — P-values by Gene and Condition

P-values extracted automatically from the interaction GLM results above. Colour scale: red = low p-value (significant), blue = high p-value (non-significant). Significance markers: *** p < 0.001 · ** p < 0.01 · * p < 0.05. Labels use scientific notation when p < 0.001, otherwise 3 decimal places. Genes are ordered by their maximum p-value across conditions (most stable at top). Palette: RdYlBu (colorblind-friendly, as recommended by Lin).

Q-Q Plots of Residuals

Residual Histograms

1.4.2. Model Comparison — AIC (Additive vs. Interaction)

Interpretation: ΔAIC > 2 favours the interaction model; |ΔAIC| < 2 indicates equivalent support and the simpler (additive) model is preferred by parsimony. This table is provided as supplementary justification for model selection.

AIC comparison — Additive vs Interaction Ct GLMs
Gene AIC_Additive AIC_Interaction Delta_AIC Preferred
ACT1 274.32 269.70 4.62 Interaction
ACT2 284.58 281.24 3.34 Interaction
AK 284.21 288.15 -3.94 Additive
ANX 278.56 286.77 -8.21 Additive
eEfa1 284.10 286.29 -2.19 Additive
pAbp 286.98 294.58 -7.60 Additive
RPL3 260.07 267.03 -6.96 Additive
GAPDH 297.25 302.46 -5.21 Additive
UCCR 263.43 265.67 -2.24 Additive

📊 Automatic AIC summary: The additive model was preferred in 7 of 9 genes (|ΔAIC| > 2), indicating that interactions between Treatment, Sex, and Tissue do not explain substantial additional variance in Ct values. This supports interpreting the effects of each factor independently.

2. Relative Expression Analysis (EGR)

Context: EGR values result from RefFinder / geNorm rankings. The Quality variable classifies genes as “worst” or “best” reference genes based on their stability scores. The goal is to compare how expression results change when normalizing with each set.

2.1. EGR Summary

Summary statistics — EGR data
Gene Quality Treatment Sex Tissue EGR Log_EGR
HSP90:108 best :54 Cold :36 Female:54 A:36 Min. : 0.001 Min. :-3.0211
NA worst:54 Control:36 Male :54 H:36 1st Qu.: 0.488 1st Qu.:-0.3119
NA NA Heat :36 NA T:36 Median : 3.084 Median : 0.4890
NA NA NA NA NA Mean : 93.813 Mean : 0.4817
NA NA NA NA NA 3rd Qu.: 20.310 3rd Qu.: 1.3076
NA NA NA NA NA Max. :3969.092 Max. : 3.5987

Quality F_value Pr_F Signif
best 1.1535e+00 3.473e-01 ns
worst 1.1665e+00 3.374e-01 ns

Quality F_value Pr_F Signif
best 3.482e-01 9.885e-01 ns
worst 4.145e-01 9.724e-01 ns

2.4. Barplots — Worst vs Best Reference Genes

2.5. Log(EGR) GLM Analysis

Family auto-selected: gaussian(link = "identity"). Log(EGR) contains negative values (range: -3.02 – 3.6), which are incompatible with Gamma distributions (requiring Y > 0 strictly). No AIC family comparison is needed — Gaussian is the only valid candidate. This is equivalent to a standard linear model; Type II LR tests are used for effects.

2.5.1. Interaction Model — Treatment * Sex * Tissue

ANOVA Results (Type II F-Test)

Interaction GLM — ANOVA Type II LR test (log(EGR) ~ Treatment * Sex * Tissue)
Quality Term Df LR_Chisq Pr_Chi Signif
best Treatment 2e+00 1.8216e+01 1.000e-04 ***
best Sex 1e+00 1.4886e+01 1.000e-04 ***
best Tissue 2e+00 7.3600e-01 6.922e-01 ns
best Treatment:Sex 2e+00 8.2560e+00 1.610e-02
best Treatment:Tissue 4e+00 4.8850e+00 2.993e-01 ns
best Sex:Tissue 2e+00 6.3900e-01 7.267e-01 ns
best Treatment:Sex:Tissue 4e+00 8.1100e-01 9.370e-01 ns
worst Treatment 2e+00 7.2920e+00 2.610e-02
worst Sex 1e+00 1.2766e+01 4.000e-04 ***
worst Tissue 2e+00 4.4720e+00 1.069e-01 ns
worst Treatment:Sex 2e+00 6.9700e+00 3.060e-02
worst Treatment:Tissue 4e+00 3.6930e+00 4.491e-01 ns
worst Sex:Tissue 2e+00 2.8100e+00 2.453e-01 ns
worst Treatment:Sex:Tissue 4e+00 1.7050e+00 7.898e-01 ns

Q-Q Plots of Residuals

Residual Histograms

2.5.2. Model Comparison — AIC (Additive vs. Interaction)

AIC comparison — Additive vs Interaction log(EGR) GLMs
Quality AIC_Additive AIC_Interaction Delta_AIC Preferred
best 144.68 150.31 -5.63 Additive
worst 170.69 175.69 -5.00 Additive

📊 Automatic AIC summary: The additive model was preferred for all 2 gene quality groups (|ΔAIC| > 2), indicating that interactions between Treatment, Sex, and Tissue do not improve the explanation of log(EGR) variance. Effects of each factor can be interpreted independently.
